425	    def _add_plot_annotations(
426	        self,
427	        ax: Axes,
428	        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
429	        valid_x: List[float],
430	        y_data: List[float],
431	        valid_concurrency: List[int],
432	        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
433	    ) -> None:
434	        """Add annotations to plot points."""
435	        for x_val, y_val, c_val in zip(
436	            valid_x, y_data, valid_concurrency, strict=False
437	        ):
438	            # Show y-value when x-axis is concurrency, otherwise show concurrency
439	            if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
440	                # Format y-value with appropriate precision based on time unit
441	                if metrics_time_unit == "ms":
442	                    annotation_text = f"{y_val:.1f}"
443	                else:
444	                    annotation_text = f"{y_val:.2f}"
445	            else:
446	                annotation_text = f"{c_val}"
447	
448	            ax.annotate(
449	                annotation_text,
450	                (x_val, y_val),
451	                fontsize=8,
452	                alpha=1.0,
453	                xytext=(4, 4),
454	                textcoords="offset points",
455	                ha="left",
456	                bbox=dict(
457	                    boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
458	                    facecolor="white",
459	                    alpha=0.1,
460	                    edgecolor="none",
461	                ),
462	            )

464	    def _plot_multi_line_metric(
465	        self,
466	        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
467	        ax: Axes,
468	        concurrency_data: Dict[int, MetricsData],
469	        concurrency_levels: List[int],
470	        label: str,
471	        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
472	        source_time_unit: str = "s",
473	    ) -> None:
474	        """Plot multiple lines on the same subplot."""
475	        # Get colors from tab10 colormap
476	        cmap = plt.get_cmap("tab10")
477	        colors = [cmap(i) for i in range(10)]  # Get first 10 colors
478	        linestyles = ["-", "--", "-.", ":"]
479	
480	        x_data: List[Any] = []
481	        for c in concurrency_levels:
482	            try:
483	                scenario_data = concurrency_data[c]
484	                if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
485	                    x_data.append(None)
486	                    continue
487	                metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
488	                x_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(metrics, plot_spec.x_field)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
489	                if x_val is not None:
490	                    x_data.append(x_val)
491	                else:
492	                    x_data.append(None)
493	            except (AttributeError, KeyError):
494	                x_data.append(None)
495	
496	        # Plot each Y-field as a separate line
497	        for i, y_field_spec in enumerate(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()):
498	            y_data = []
499	            valid_x = []
500	            valid_concurrency = []
501	
502	            for j, c in enumerate(concurrency_levels):
503	                if x_data[j] is None:
504	                    continue
505	
506	                try:
507	                    scenario_data = concurrency_data[c]
508	                    if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
509	                        continue
510	                    metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
511	                    y_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(
512	                        metrics, y_field_spec.field
513	                    )  # type: ignore[arg-type]
514	
515	                    if y_val is not None:
516	                        # Convert time values if this is a latency field
517	                        # and if source and target units are different
518	                        is_latency = TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(
519	                            y_field_spec.field
520	                        )
521	                        if is_latency and source_time_unit != metrics_time_unit:
522	                            y_val = TimeUnitConverter.convert_value(
523	                                y_val, source_time_unit, metrics_time_unit
524	                            )
525	                        y_data.append(y_val)
526	                        # Use evenly spaced positions for concurrency, actual values
527	                        # otherwise
528	                        if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
529	                            valid_x.append(j)  # Even spacing: 0, 1, 2, 3...
530	                        else:
531	                            valid_x.append(x_data[j])  # Actual values
532	                        valid_concurrency.append(c)
533	
534	                except (AttributeError, KeyError) as e:
535	                    logger.warning(
536	                        f"Missing data for field {y_field_spec.field}, "
537	                        f"concurrency {c}: {e}"
538	                    )
539	                    continue
540	
541	            if not y_data:
542	                logger.warning(f"No valid data for field: {y_field_spec.field}")
543	                continue
544	
545	            # Determine line styling
546	            color = y_field_spec.color or colors[i % len(colors)]
547	            linestyle = y_field_spec.linestyle or linestyles[i % len(linestyles)]
548	            line_label = y_field_spec.label or self._generate_label(
549	                y_field_spec.field, metrics_time_unit
550	            )
551	
552	            # For multi-line plots, keep labels clean
553	            full_label = line_label
554	
555	            # Plot the line
556	            if plot_spec.plot_type == "line":
557	                ax.plot(
558	                    valid_x,
559	                    y_data,
560	                    linestyle=linestyle,
561	                    color=color,
562	                    marker="o",
563	                    label=full_label,
564	                    markersize=4,
565	                )
566	                self._add_plot_annotations(
567	                    ax,
568	                    plot_spec,
569	                    valid_x,
570	                    y_data,
571	                    valid_concurrency,
572	                    metrics_time_unit,
573	                )
574	            elif plot_spec.plot_type == "scatter":
575	                ax.scatter(valid_x, y_data, color=color, label=full_label)
576	                self._add_plot_annotations(
577	                    ax,
578	                    plot_spec,
579	                    valid_x,
580	                    y_data,
581	                    valid_concurrency,
582	                    metrics_time_unit,
583	                )
584	            elif plot_spec.plot_type == "bar":
585	                # For bar plots with multiple fields, use grouped bars
586	                bar_width = 0.8 / len(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs())
587	                x_offset = (
588	                    i - len(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()) / 2 + 0.5
589	                ) * bar_width
590	                ax.bar(
591	                    [x + x_offset for x in valid_x],
592	                    y_data,
593	                    width=bar_width,
594	                    color=color,
595	                    label=full_label,
596	                )
597	
598	        # Set labels and title
599	        ax.set_xlabel(
600	            plot_spec.x_label
601	            or self._generate_label(plot_spec.x_field, metrics_time_unit)
602	        )
603	
604	        # Update y-axis label and title with correct time unit if this is a latency plot
605	        y_label = plot_spec.y_label or "Value"
606	        title = plot_spec.title
607	
608	        # Check if any of the y-fields are latency fields
609	        # to determine if we need time unit conversion
610	        has_latency_fields = any(
611	            TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(spec.field)
612	            for spec in plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
613	        )
614	
615	        if has_latency_fields:
616	            y_label = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(y_label, metrics_time_unit)
617	            title = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(title, metrics_time_unit)
618	
619	        ax.set_ylabel(y_label)
620	        ax.set_title(title)
621	        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
622	
623	        # Position legend outside plot area for multi-line plots to avoid overlap
624	        ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left", fontsize="small")
625	
626	        # Apply y-axis scale from config or default based on field name
627	        if plot_spec.y_scale:
628	            # Use explicit scale from config
629	            if plot_spec.y_scale == "log":
630	                ax.set_yscale("log", base=10)
631	            # 'linear' is matplotlib default, no need to set explicitly
632	        else:
633	            # Backward compatibility: apply log scale for TTFT fields if not specified
634	            y_field_specs = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
635	            if any("ttft" in spec.field.lower() for spec in y_field_specs):
636	                ax.set_yscale("log", base=10)
637	
638	        # Handle concurrency x-axis formatting - evenly spaced ticks
639	        if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
640	            ax.set_xticks(range(len(concurrency_levels)))
641	            ax.set_xticklabels([str(level) for level in concurrency_levels])
642	
643	    def _save_individual_subplots_multiline(
644	        self, axs: Any, experiment_folder: str, output_file_prefix: str
645	    ) -> None:
646	        """
647	        Custom subplot saving that properly handles multi-line plots.
648	        """
649	
650	        for ax in axs.flat:
651	            title = ax.get_title()
652	            if not title:  # Skip empty subplots
653	                continue
654	
655	            sanitized_title = sanitize_string(title)
656	
657	            fig_temp, ax_temp = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
658	
659	            # Copy all line plots with proper styling
660	            for line in ax.get_lines():
661	                ax_temp.plot(
662	                    line.get_xdata(),
663	                    line.get_ydata(),
664	                    label=line.get_label(),
665	                    marker=line.get_marker(),
666	                    linestyle=line.get_linestyle(),
667	                    color=line.get_color(),
668	                    markersize=line.get_markersize(),
669	                    alpha=line.get_alpha() or 1.0,
670	                )
671	
672	            # Copy annotations - handle both multi-line and single-line plots
673	            # For all plots (multi-line and single-line), copy existing annotations
674	            for annotation in ax.texts:
675	                # Get the actual xy coordinates the annotation is pointing to
676	                xy_coords = (
677	                    annotation.xy
678	                    if hasattr(annotation, "xy")
679	                    else annotation.get_position()
680	                )
681	
682	                # Get the original annotation properties
683	                original_xytext = (
684	                    annotation.xytext if hasattr(annotation, "xytext") else (4, 4)
685	                )
686	                original_textcoords = (
687	                    annotation.textcoords
688	                    if hasattr(annotation, "textcoords")
689	                    else "offset points"
690	                )
691	
692	                ax_temp.annotate(
693	                    annotation.get_text(),
694	                    xy=xy_coords,  # Use the actual data coordinates
695	                    fontsize=annotation.get_fontsize(),
696	                    ha=annotation.get_ha(),
697	                    va=annotation.get_va(),
698	                    alpha=annotation.get_alpha() or 1.0,
699	                    xytext=original_xytext,
700	                    textcoords=original_textcoords,
701	                    bbox=dict(
702	                        boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
703	                        facecolor="white",
704	                        alpha=0.1,
705	                        edgecolor="none",
706	                    ),
707	                )
708	
709	            # Copy axis properties
710	            ax_temp.set_title(title)
711	            ax_temp.set_xlabel(ax.get_xlabel())
712	            ax_temp.set_ylabel(ax.get_ylabel())
713	
714	            # Copy ticks and labels
715	            ax_temp.set_xticks(ax.get_xticks())
716	            ax_temp.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels())
717	            ax_temp.set_yticks(ax.get_yticks())
718	            ax_temp.set_yticklabels(ax.get_yticklabels())
719	
720	            # Copy axis limits
721	            ax_temp.set_xlim(ax.get_xlim())
722	            ax_temp.set_ylim(ax.get_ylim())
723	
724	            # Copy scale settings
725	            if ax.get_yscale() == "log":
726	                ax_temp.set_yscale("log", base=10)
727	            if ax.get_xscale() == "log":
728	                ax_temp.set_xscale("log", base=10)
729	
730	            # Copy grid
731	            ax_temp.grid(
732	                ax.get_xgridlines()[0].get_visible() if ax.get_xgridlines() else True,
733	                alpha=0.3,
734	            )
735	            ax_temp.minorticks_on()
736	
737	            # Handle legend properly for multi-line plots
738	            handles, labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
739	            if handles and labels:
740	                # Clean up labels and position legend properly
741	                cleaned_labels = []
742	                for label in labels:
743	                    # Remove any scenario prefixes for cleaner individual plots
744	                    if " - " in label:
745	                        label = label.split(" - ")[-1]
746	                    cleaned_labels.append(label)
747	
748	                ax_temp.legend(
749	                    handles,
750	                    cleaned_labels,
751	                    fontsize="small",
752	                    bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1),
753	                    loc="upper left",
754	                )
755	
756	            plt.tight_layout()
757	            # Adjust for external legend
758	            if handles and labels:
759	                plt.subplots_adjust(right=0.85)
760	
761	            output_file = os.path.join(
762	                experiment_folder, f"{output_file_prefix}_{sanitized_title}.png"
763	            )
764	            fig_temp.savefig(output_file, bbox_inches="tight")
765	            plt.close(fig_temp)
766	            logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")
767	
768	    def _generate_label(self, field_path: str, metrics_time_unit: str = "s") -> str:
769	        """Generate a human-readable label from field path."""
770	        parts = field_path.split(".")
771	        metric_name_for_latency_check = field_path
772	
773	        if parts[0] == "stats":
774	            metric = parts[1].replace("_", " ").title()
775	            metric_name_for_latency_check = parts[1]
776	            if len(parts) > 2:
777	                stat = parts[2].upper()
778	                base_label = f"{metric} ({stat})"
779	            else:
780	                base_label = metric
781	        else:
782	            base_label = field_path.replace("_", " ").title()
783	
784	        if TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(metric_name_for_latency_check):
785	            base_label = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(base_label, metrics_time_unit)
786	
787	        return base_label
788	
789	    def _finalize_and_save_plots(
790	        self,
791	        axs: Any,
792	        fig: Figure,
793	        labels: List[str],
794	        experiment_folder: str,
795	        output_file_prefix: str,
796	    ) -> None:
797	        """Finalize and save plots."""
798	
799	        # Hide unused subplots
800	        layout = self.config.layout
801	        used_positions = {plot.position for plot in self.config.plots}
802	
803	        for row in range(layout.rows):
804	            for col in range(layout.cols):
805	                if (row, col) not in used_positions:
806	                    axs[row, col].set_visible(False)
807	
808	        # Save individual subplots (custom version for multi-line plots)
809	        self._save_individual_subplots_multiline(
810	            axs, experiment_folder, output_file_prefix
811	        )
812	
813	        # For multi-line plots, legends are already positioned on individual subplots
814	        # Only add global legend for single-line plots
815	        has_multi_line = any(plot.is_multi_line() for plot in self.config.plots)
816	
817	        if not has_multi_line:
818	            fig.legend(labels=labels, loc="lower center", ncol=3, fancybox=True)
819	            plt.tight_layout()
820	            plt.subplots_adjust(bottom=0.1)
821	        else:
822	            plt.tight_layout()
823	            # Adjust layout to accommodate external legends
824	            plt.subplots_adjust(right=0.85)
825	
826	        output_file = os.path.join(
827	            experiment_folder,
828	            f"{output_file_prefix}_combined_plots_{layout.rows}x{layout.cols}.png",
829	        )
830	        logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")
831	        plt.savefig(output_file)
832	        plt.close()
833

835	def plot_experiment_data_flexible(
836	    run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
837	    group_key: str,
838	    experiment_folder: str,
839	    plot_config: Optional[PlotConfig] = None,
840	    metrics_time_unit: Optional[str] = None,
841	) -> None:
842	    """
843	    Plot experiment data using flexible configuration.
844	
845	    Args:
846	        run_data_list: List of experiment data tuples
847	        group_key: Key to group data by
848	        experiment_folder: Output folder path
849	        plot_config: Plot configuration (uses default if None)
850	        metrics_time_unit: Time unit to use for plotting ('s' or 'ms').
851	            If None, extracts from metadata.
852	    """
853	    # Use CLI metrics_time_unit parameter if provided, otherwise extract
854	    # from experiment metadata
855	    if metrics_time_unit is None:
856	        metrics_time_unit = (
857	            run_data_list[0][0].metrics_time_unit if run_data_list else "s"
858	        )
859	
860	    if plot_config is None:
861	        plot_config = PlotConfigManager.load_preset("2x4_default", metrics_time_unit)
862

864	    generator.generate_plots(
865	        run_data_list, group_key, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit
866	    )

868	
869	def validate_plot_config_with_data(
870	    config: PlotConfig,

872	) -> List[str]:

875

893	        sample_agg_metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
894	    except (IndexError, KeyError) as e:

896	        return errors

900	        # Validate X field path
901	        if not PlotConfigManager.validate_field_path(
902	            plot_spec.x_field,
903	            sample_agg_metrics,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
904	        ):
905	            errors.append(f"Plot {i + 1}: Invalid x_field '{plot_spec.x_field}'")
906

909	            y_field_specs = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
910	            for j, y_field_spec in enumerate(y_field_specs):
911	                if not PlotConfigManager.validate_field_path(

914	                ):

918	                        )

"""Flexible plotting system supporting user-defined plot configurations."""

import os
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.axes import Axes
from matplotlib.figure import Figure

from genai_bench.analysis.experiment_loader import ExperimentMetrics, MetricsData
from genai_bench.analysis.plot_config import PlotConfig, PlotConfigManager, PlotSpec
from genai_bench.analysis.plot_report import (
    get_scenario_data,
    plot_error_rates,
    plot_graph,
)
from genai_bench.logging import init_logger
from genai_bench.protocol import ExperimentMetadata
from genai_bench.time_units import TimeUnitConverter
from genai_bench.utils import sanitize_string

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class FlexiblePlotGenerator:
    """Generates plots using flexible configuration."""

    def __init__(self, config: PlotConfig):
        """Initialize with plot configuration and time unit."""
        self.config = config

    def generate_plots(
        self,
        run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
        group_key: str,
        experiment_folder: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Generate plots based on configuration."""
        # Extract source time unit from experiment metadata
        source_time_unit = (
            run_data_list[0][0].metrics_time_unit if run_data_list else "s"
        )

        if group_key == "none":
            self._plot_single_analysis(
                run_data_list, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit, source_time_unit
            )
        elif group_key == "traffic_scenario":
            self._plot_by_scenario(
                run_data_list, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit, source_time_unit
            )
        else:
            self._plot_by_group(
                run_data_list,
                group_key,
                experiment_folder,
                metrics_time_unit,
                source_time_unit,
            )

    def _plot_single_analysis(
        self,
        run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
        experiment_folder: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot for single scenario analysis without grouping - plots ALL scenarios."""
        if not run_data_list:
            logger.warning("No experiment data found for single analysis")
            return

        # Get the first experiment
        metadata, experiment_metrics = run_data_list[0]
        all_scenarios = list(experiment_metrics.keys())

        logger.info(
            f"Single scenario analysis mode - plotting all {len(all_scenarios)} "
            f"scenarios: {all_scenarios}"
        )

        # Plot each scenario individually for clean multi-line analysis
        for scenario in all_scenarios:
            concurrency_data = experiment_metrics[scenario]
            concurrency_levels = sorted(concurrency_data.keys())

            fig, axs = self._create_figure()
            fig.suptitle(f"Single Scenario Analysis: {scenario}", fontsize=14)

            # Single scenario = no grouping, perfect for multi-line plots
            # Pass empty label since we're not grouping
            self._plot_metrics(
                axs,
                [concurrency_data],
                {"": concurrency_levels},
                [""],
                metrics_time_unit,
                source_time_unit,
            )
            self._finalize_and_save_plots(
                axs,
                fig,
                [""],
                experiment_folder,
                f"single_analysis_{sanitize_string(scenario)}",
            )

    def _plot_by_scenario(
        self,
        run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
        experiment_folder: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot grouped by traffic scenario."""
        label_to_concurrency_map, concurrency_data_list, labels = get_scenario_data(
            run_data_list
        )

        fig, axs = self._create_figure()
        fig.suptitle("Grouped by Traffic Scenario", fontsize=14)

        self._plot_metrics(
            axs,
            concurrency_data_list,
            label_to_concurrency_map,
            labels,
            metrics_time_unit,
            source_time_unit,
        )
        self._finalize_and_save_plots(
            axs, fig, labels, experiment_folder, "traffic_scenario"
        )

    def _plot_by_group(
        self,
        run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
        group_key: str,
        experiment_folder: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot grouped by specified group key."""
        from genai_bench.analysis.plot_report import (
            extract_traffic_scenarios,
            get_group_data,
        )

        traffic_scenarios = extract_traffic_scenarios(run_data_list)
        for traffic_scenario in traffic_scenarios:
            fig, axs = self._create_figure()
            fig.suptitle(f"Traffic Scenario: {traffic_scenario}", fontsize=14)

            label_to_concurrency_map, concurrency_data_list, labels = get_group_data(
                run_data_list, traffic_scenario, group_key
            )

            self._plot_metrics(
                axs,
                concurrency_data_list,
                label_to_concurrency_map,
                labels,
                metrics_time_unit,
                source_time_unit,
            )
            self._finalize_and_save_plots(
                axs,
                fig,
                labels,
                experiment_folder,
                f"{sanitize_string(traffic_scenario)}_group_by_{group_key}",
            )

    def _create_figure(self) -> Tuple[Figure, Any]:
        """Create figure with configured layout."""
        layout = self.config.layout
        figsize = layout.figsize or (8 * layout.cols, 6 * layout.rows)

        fig, axs = plt.subplots(layout.rows, layout.cols, figsize=figsize)

        # Ensure axs is always 2D array for consistent indexing
        if layout.rows == 1 and layout.cols == 1:
            axs = np.array([[axs]])
        elif layout.rows == 1:
            axs = axs.reshape(1, -1)
        elif layout.cols == 1:
            axs = axs.reshape(-1, 1)

        return fig, axs

    def _plot_metrics(
        self,
        axs: Any,
        concurrency_data_list: List[Dict[int, MetricsData]],
        label_to_concurrency_map: Dict[str, List[int]],
        labels: List[str],
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot metrics based on configuration."""
        # Check if we have multi-line plots with multiple scenarios/groups
        has_multi_line = any(plot.is_multi_line() for plot in self.config.plots)
        has_multiple_groups = len(labels) > 1

        if has_multi_line and has_multiple_groups:
            logger.warning(
                f"Multi-line plots detected with {len(labels)} groups/scenarios. "
                f"Multi-line plots work best with single scenarios. "
                f"Converting to single-line plots for better visualization."
            )
            # Convert multi-line plots to single-line plots automatically
            self._plot_metrics_single_line_fallback(
                axs,
                concurrency_data_list,
                label_to_concurrency_map,
                labels,
                metrics_time_unit,
                source_time_unit,
            )
            return

        for i, concurrency_data in enumerate(concurrency_data_list):
            concurrency_levels = label_to_concurrency_map[labels[i]]

            for plot_spec in self.config.plots:
                self._plot_single_metric(
                    plot_spec=plot_spec,
                    ax=axs[plot_spec.position[0], plot_spec.position[1]],
                    concurrency_data=concurrency_data,
                    concurrency_levels=concurrency_levels,
                    label=labels[i],
                    metrics_time_unit=metrics_time_unit,
                    source_time_unit=source_time_unit,
                )

    def _plot_metrics_single_line_fallback(
        self,
        axs: Any,
        concurrency_data_list: List[Dict[int, MetricsData]],
        label_to_concurrency_map: Dict[str, List[int]],
        labels: List[str],
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Fallback to single-line plotting when multi-line conflicts with grouping."""
        logger.info(
            "Converting multi-line plots to single-line plots for better "
            "visualization with multiple groups"
        )

        for i, concurrency_data in enumerate(concurrency_data_list):
            concurrency_levels = label_to_concurrency_map[labels[i]]

            for plot_spec in self.config.plots:
                if plot_spec.is_multi_line():
                    # For multi-line plots, just use the first Y-field
                    y_field_specs = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
                    first_field = y_field_specs[0]

                    # Create a temporary single-line plot spec
                    from genai_bench.analysis.plot_config import PlotSpec

                    single_line_spec = PlotSpec(
                        title=f"{plot_spec.title} "
                        f"({first_field.label or self._generate_label(first_field.field, metrics_time_unit)})",  # noqa: E501
                        x_field=plot_spec.x_field,
                        y_field=first_field.field,
                        x_label=plot_spec.x_label,
                        y_fields=None,
                        position=plot_spec.position,
                        y_scale=plot_spec.y_scale,
                        y_label=plot_spec.y_label,
                        plot_type=plot_spec.plot_type,
                    )

                    self._plot_single_line_metric(
                        plot_spec=single_line_spec,
                        ax=axs[plot_spec.position[0], plot_spec.position[1]],
                        concurrency_data=concurrency_data,
                        concurrency_levels=concurrency_levels,
                        label=labels[i],
                        metrics_time_unit=metrics_time_unit,
                        source_time_unit=source_time_unit,
                    )
                else:
                    # Regular single-line plot
                    self._plot_single_metric(
                        plot_spec=plot_spec,
                        ax=axs[plot_spec.position[0], plot_spec.position[1]],
                        concurrency_data=concurrency_data,
                        concurrency_levels=concurrency_levels,
                        label=labels[i],
                        metrics_time_unit=metrics_time_unit,
                        source_time_unit=source_time_unit,
                    )

    def _plot_single_metric(
        self,
        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
        ax: Axes,
        concurrency_data: Dict[int, MetricsData],
        concurrency_levels: List[int],
        label: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot a single metric based on plot specification."""
        try:
            if plot_spec.is_multi_line():
                self._plot_multi_line_metric(
                    plot_spec,
                    ax,
                    concurrency_data,
                    concurrency_levels,
                    label,
                    metrics_time_unit,
                    source_time_unit,
                )
            else:
                self._plot_single_line_metric(
                    plot_spec,
                    ax,
                    concurrency_data,
                    concurrency_levels,
                    label,
                    metrics_time_unit,
                    source_time_unit,
                )

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error plotting {plot_spec.title}: {e}")
            ax.text(
                0.5,
                0.5,
                f"Error: {str(e)}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                transform=ax.transAxes,
            )

    def _plot_single_line_metric(
        self,
        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
        ax: Axes,
        concurrency_data: Dict[int, MetricsData],
        concurrency_levels: List[int],
        label: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot a single line metric (original behavior)."""
        # Extract data using field paths
        x_data = []
        y_data = []
        valid_concurrency = []

        y_field_spec = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()[0]  # Single field

        for c in concurrency_levels:
            try:
                scenario_data = concurrency_data[c]
                if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
                    continue
                metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]

                x_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(metrics, plot_spec.x_field)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
                y_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(metrics, y_field_spec.field)  # type: ignore[arg-type]

                if x_val is not None and y_val is not None:
                    x_data.append(x_val)
                    y_data.append(y_val)
                    valid_concurrency.append(c)

            except (AttributeError, KeyError) as e:
                logger.warning(f"Missing data for concurrency {c}: {e}")
                continue

        if not x_data or not y_data:
            logger.warning(f"No valid data for plot: {plot_spec.title}")
            return

        # Convert time data values if this is a latency field
        is_latency = TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(y_field_spec.field)

        if is_latency and source_time_unit != metrics_time_unit:
            y_data = [
                TimeUnitConverter.convert_value(
                    val, source_time_unit, metrics_time_unit
                )
                for val in y_data
            ]

        # Handle special error rate plot
        if y_field_spec.field == "error_rate" and plot_spec.plot_type == "bar":
            plot_error_rates(ax, concurrency_data, concurrency_levels, label)
        else:
            # Apply time unit conversion to labels and title if this is a latency field
            y_label = plot_spec.y_label or self._generate_label(
                y_field_spec.field, metrics_time_unit
            )
            title = plot_spec.title

            # Convert labels to correct time unit if this is a latency field
            if TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(y_field_spec.field):
                y_label = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(y_label, metrics_time_unit)
                title = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(title, metrics_time_unit)

            # Use existing plot_graph function
            plot_graph(
                ax=ax,
                x_data=x_data,
                y_data=y_data,
                x_label=plot_spec.x_label
                or self._generate_label(plot_spec.x_field, metrics_time_unit),
                y_label=y_label,
                title=title,
                concurrency_levels=valid_concurrency,
                label=label,
                plot_type=plot_spec.plot_type,
                metrics_time_unit=metrics_time_unit,
            )

    def _add_plot_annotations(
        self,
        ax: Axes,
        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
        valid_x: List[float],
        y_data: List[float],
        valid_concurrency: List[int],
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Add annotations to plot points."""
        for x_val, y_val, c_val in zip(
            valid_x, y_data, valid_concurrency, strict=False
        ):
            # Show y-value when x-axis is concurrency, otherwise show concurrency
            if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
                # Format y-value with appropriate precision based on time unit
                if metrics_time_unit == "ms":
                    annotation_text = f"{y_val:.1f}"
                else:
                    annotation_text = f"{y_val:.2f}"
            else:
                annotation_text = f"{c_val}"

            ax.annotate(
                annotation_text,
                (x_val, y_val),
                fontsize=8,
                alpha=1.0,
                xytext=(4, 4),
                textcoords="offset points",
                ha="left",
                bbox=dict(
                    boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
                    facecolor="white",
                    alpha=0.1,
                    edgecolor="none",
                ),
            )

    def _plot_multi_line_metric(
        self,
        plot_spec: PlotSpec,
        ax: Axes,
        concurrency_data: Dict[int, MetricsData],
        concurrency_levels: List[int],
        label: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
        source_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Plot multiple lines on the same subplot."""
        # Get colors from tab10 colormap
        cmap = plt.get_cmap("tab10")
        colors = [cmap(i) for i in range(10)]  # Get first 10 colors
        linestyles = ["-", "--", "-.", ":"]

        x_data: List[Any] = []
        for c in concurrency_levels:
            try:
                scenario_data = concurrency_data[c]
                if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
                    x_data.append(None)
                    continue
                metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
                x_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(metrics, plot_spec.x_field)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
                if x_val is not None:
                    x_data.append(x_val)
                else:
                    x_data.append(None)
            except (AttributeError, KeyError):
                x_data.append(None)

        # Plot each Y-field as a separate line
        for i, y_field_spec in enumerate(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()):
            y_data = []
            valid_x = []
            valid_concurrency = []

            for j, c in enumerate(concurrency_levels):
                if x_data[j] is None:
                    continue

                try:
                    scenario_data = concurrency_data[c]
                    if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
                        continue
                    metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
                    y_val = PlotConfigManager.get_field_value(
                        metrics, y_field_spec.field
                    )  # type: ignore[arg-type]

                    if y_val is not None:
                        # Convert time values if this is a latency field
                        # and if source and target units are different
                        is_latency = TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(
                            y_field_spec.field
                        )
                        if is_latency and source_time_unit != metrics_time_unit:
                            y_val = TimeUnitConverter.convert_value(
                                y_val, source_time_unit, metrics_time_unit
                            )
                        y_data.append(y_val)
                        # Use evenly spaced positions for concurrency, actual values
                        # otherwise
                        if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
                            valid_x.append(j)  # Even spacing: 0, 1, 2, 3...
                        else:
                            valid_x.append(x_data[j])  # Actual values
                        valid_concurrency.append(c)

                except (AttributeError, KeyError) as e:
                    logger.warning(
                        f"Missing data for field {y_field_spec.field}, "
                        f"concurrency {c}: {e}"
                    )
                    continue

            if not y_data:
                logger.warning(f"No valid data for field: {y_field_spec.field}")
                continue

            # Determine line styling
            color = y_field_spec.color or colors[i % len(colors)]
            linestyle = y_field_spec.linestyle or linestyles[i % len(linestyles)]
            line_label = y_field_spec.label or self._generate_label(
                y_field_spec.field, metrics_time_unit
            )

            # For multi-line plots, keep labels clean
            full_label = line_label

            # Plot the line
            if plot_spec.plot_type == "line":
                ax.plot(
                    valid_x,
                    y_data,
                    linestyle=linestyle,
                    color=color,
                    marker="o",
                    label=full_label,
                    markersize=4,
                )
                self._add_plot_annotations(
                    ax,
                    plot_spec,
                    valid_x,
                    y_data,
                    valid_concurrency,
                    metrics_time_unit,
                )
            elif plot_spec.plot_type == "scatter":
                ax.scatter(valid_x, y_data, color=color, label=full_label)
                self._add_plot_annotations(
                    ax,
                    plot_spec,
                    valid_x,
                    y_data,
                    valid_concurrency,
                    metrics_time_unit,
                )
            elif plot_spec.plot_type == "bar":
                # For bar plots with multiple fields, use grouped bars
                bar_width = 0.8 / len(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs())
                x_offset = (
                    i - len(plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()) / 2 + 0.5
                ) * bar_width
                ax.bar(
                    [x + x_offset for x in valid_x],
                    y_data,
                    width=bar_width,
                    color=color,
                    label=full_label,
                )

        # Set labels and title
        ax.set_xlabel(
            plot_spec.x_label
            or self._generate_label(plot_spec.x_field, metrics_time_unit)
        )

        # Update y-axis label and title with correct time unit if this is a latency plot
        y_label = plot_spec.y_label or "Value"
        title = plot_spec.title

        # Check if any of the y-fields are latency fields
        # to determine if we need time unit conversion
        has_latency_fields = any(
            TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(spec.field)
            for spec in plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
        )

        if has_latency_fields:
            y_label = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(y_label, metrics_time_unit)
            title = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(title, metrics_time_unit)

        ax.set_ylabel(y_label)
        ax.set_title(title)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

        # Position legend outside plot area for multi-line plots to avoid overlap
        ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left", fontsize="small")

        # Apply y-axis scale from config or default based on field name
        if plot_spec.y_scale:
            # Use explicit scale from config
            if plot_spec.y_scale == "log":
                ax.set_yscale("log", base=10)
            # 'linear' is matplotlib default, no need to set explicitly
        else:
            # Backward compatibility: apply log scale for TTFT fields if not specified
            y_field_specs = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
            if any("ttft" in spec.field.lower() for spec in y_field_specs):
                ax.set_yscale("log", base=10)

        # Handle concurrency x-axis formatting - evenly spaced ticks
        if plot_spec.x_field == "num_concurrency":
            ax.set_xticks(range(len(concurrency_levels)))
            ax.set_xticklabels([str(level) for level in concurrency_levels])

    def _save_individual_subplots_multiline(
        self, axs: Any, experiment_folder: str, output_file_prefix: str
    ) -> None:
        """
        Custom subplot saving that properly handles multi-line plots.
        """

        for ax in axs.flat:
            title = ax.get_title()
            if not title:  # Skip empty subplots
                continue

            sanitized_title = sanitize_string(title)

            fig_temp, ax_temp = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))

            # Copy all line plots with proper styling
            for line in ax.get_lines():
                ax_temp.plot(
                    line.get_xdata(),
                    line.get_ydata(),
                    label=line.get_label(),
                    marker=line.get_marker(),
                    linestyle=line.get_linestyle(),
                    color=line.get_color(),
                    markersize=line.get_markersize(),
                    alpha=line.get_alpha() or 1.0,
                )

            # Copy annotations - handle both multi-line and single-line plots
            # For all plots (multi-line and single-line), copy existing annotations
            for annotation in ax.texts:
                # Get the actual xy coordinates the annotation is pointing to
                xy_coords = (
                    annotation.xy
                    if hasattr(annotation, "xy")
                    else annotation.get_position()
                )

                # Get the original annotation properties
                original_xytext = (
                    annotation.xytext if hasattr(annotation, "xytext") else (4, 4)
                )
                original_textcoords = (
                    annotation.textcoords
                    if hasattr(annotation, "textcoords")
                    else "offset points"
                )

                ax_temp.annotate(
                    annotation.get_text(),
                    xy=xy_coords,  # Use the actual data coordinates
                    fontsize=annotation.get_fontsize(),
                    ha=annotation.get_ha(),
                    va=annotation.get_va(),
                    alpha=annotation.get_alpha() or 1.0,
                    xytext=original_xytext,
                    textcoords=original_textcoords,
                    bbox=dict(
                        boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
                        facecolor="white",
                        alpha=0.1,
                        edgecolor="none",
                    ),
                )

            # Copy axis properties
            ax_temp.set_title(title)
            ax_temp.set_xlabel(ax.get_xlabel())
            ax_temp.set_ylabel(ax.get_ylabel())

            # Copy ticks and labels
            ax_temp.set_xticks(ax.get_xticks())
            ax_temp.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels())
            ax_temp.set_yticks(ax.get_yticks())
            ax_temp.set_yticklabels(ax.get_yticklabels())

            # Copy axis limits
            ax_temp.set_xlim(ax.get_xlim())
            ax_temp.set_ylim(ax.get_ylim())

            # Copy scale settings
            if ax.get_yscale() == "log":
                ax_temp.set_yscale("log", base=10)
            if ax.get_xscale() == "log":
                ax_temp.set_xscale("log", base=10)

            # Copy grid
            ax_temp.grid(
                ax.get_xgridlines()[0].get_visible() if ax.get_xgridlines() else True,
                alpha=0.3,
            )
            ax_temp.minorticks_on()

            # Handle legend properly for multi-line plots
            handles, labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
            if handles and labels:
                # Clean up labels and position legend properly
                cleaned_labels = []
                for label in labels:
                    # Remove any scenario prefixes for cleaner individual plots
                    if " - " in label:
                        label = label.split(" - ")[-1]
                    cleaned_labels.append(label)

                ax_temp.legend(
                    handles,
                    cleaned_labels,
                    fontsize="small",
                    bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1),
                    loc="upper left",
                )

            plt.tight_layout()
            # Adjust for external legend
            if handles and labels:
                plt.subplots_adjust(right=0.85)

            output_file = os.path.join(
                experiment_folder, f"{output_file_prefix}_{sanitized_title}.png"
            )
            fig_temp.savefig(output_file, bbox_inches="tight")
            plt.close(fig_temp)
            logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")

    def _generate_label(self, field_path: str, metrics_time_unit: str = "s") -> str:
        """Generate a human-readable label from field path."""
        parts = field_path.split(".")
        metric_name_for_latency_check = field_path

        if parts[0] == "stats":
            metric = parts[1].replace("_", " ").title()
            metric_name_for_latency_check = parts[1]
            if len(parts) > 2:
                stat = parts[2].upper()
                base_label = f"{metric} ({stat})"
            else:
                base_label = metric
        else:
            base_label = field_path.replace("_", " ").title()

        if TimeUnitConverter.is_latency_field(metric_name_for_latency_check):
            base_label = TimeUnitConverter.get_unit_label(base_label, metrics_time_unit)

        return base_label

    def _finalize_and_save_plots(
        self,
        axs: Any,
        fig: Figure,
        labels: List[str],
        experiment_folder: str,
        output_file_prefix: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Finalize and save plots."""

        # Hide unused subplots
        layout = self.config.layout
        used_positions = {plot.position for plot in self.config.plots}

        for row in range(layout.rows):
            for col in range(layout.cols):
                if (row, col) not in used_positions:
                    axs[row, col].set_visible(False)

        # Save individual subplots (custom version for multi-line plots)
        self._save_individual_subplots_multiline(
            axs, experiment_folder, output_file_prefix
        )

        # For multi-line plots, legends are already positioned on individual subplots
        # Only add global legend for single-line plots
        has_multi_line = any(plot.is_multi_line() for plot in self.config.plots)

        if not has_multi_line:
            fig.legend(labels=labels, loc="lower center", ncol=3, fancybox=True)
            plt.tight_layout()
            plt.subplots_adjust(bottom=0.1)
        else:
            plt.tight_layout()
            # Adjust layout to accommodate external legends
            plt.subplots_adjust(right=0.85)

        output_file = os.path.join(
            experiment_folder,
            f"{output_file_prefix}_combined_plots_{layout.rows}x{layout.cols}.png",
        )
        logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")
        plt.savefig(output_file)
        plt.close()


def plot_experiment_data_flexible(
    run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
    group_key: str,
    experiment_folder: str,
    plot_config: Optional[PlotConfig] = None,
    metrics_time_unit: Optional[str] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Plot experiment data using flexible configuration.

    Args:
        run_data_list: List of experiment data tuples
        group_key: Key to group data by
        experiment_folder: Output folder path
        plot_config: Plot configuration (uses default if None)
        metrics_time_unit: Time unit to use for plotting ('s' or 'ms').
            If None, extracts from metadata.
    """
    # Use CLI metrics_time_unit parameter if provided, otherwise extract
    # from experiment metadata
    if metrics_time_unit is None:
        metrics_time_unit = (
            run_data_list[0][0].metrics_time_unit if run_data_list else "s"
        )

    if plot_config is None:
        plot_config = PlotConfigManager.load_preset("2x4_default", metrics_time_unit)

    generator = FlexiblePlotGenerator(plot_config)
    generator.generate_plots(
        run_data_list, group_key, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit
    )


def validate_plot_config_with_data(
    config: PlotConfig,
    sample_metrics: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
) -> List[str]:
    """
    Validate plot configuration against actual data.

    Returns:
        List of validation error messages (empty if valid)
    """
    errors = []

    if not sample_metrics:
        errors.append("No sample data provided for validation")
        return errors

    # Get a sample aggregated metrics object
    try:
        _, experiment_metrics = sample_metrics[0]
        first_scenario = list(experiment_metrics.keys())[0]
        first_concurrency = list(experiment_metrics[first_scenario].keys())[0]
        scenario_data = experiment_metrics[first_scenario][first_concurrency]
        if "aggregated_metrics" not in scenario_data:
            raise KeyError("aggregated_metrics not found in sample data")
        sample_agg_metrics = scenario_data["aggregated_metrics"]  # type: ignore[index]
    except (IndexError, KeyError) as e:
        errors.append(f"Cannot extract sample metrics for validation: {e}")
        return errors

    # Validate each plot specification
    for i, plot_spec in enumerate(config.plots):
        # Validate X field path
        if not PlotConfigManager.validate_field_path(
            plot_spec.x_field,
            sample_agg_metrics,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        ):
            errors.append(f"Plot {i + 1}: Invalid x_field '{plot_spec.x_field}'")

        # Validate Y field paths (single or multiple)
        try:
            y_field_specs = plot_spec.get_y_field_specs()
            for j, y_field_spec in enumerate(y_field_specs):
                if not PlotConfigManager.validate_field_path(
                    y_field_spec.field,
                    sample_agg_metrics,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
                ):
                    if len(y_field_specs) == 1:
                        errors.append(
                            f"Plot {i + 1}: Invalid y_field '{y_field_spec.field}'"
                        )
                    else:
                        errors.append(
                            f"Plot {i + 1}: Invalid y_fields[{j}]"
                            f" '{y_field_spec.field}'"
                        )
        except Exception as e:
            errors.append(f"Plot {i + 1}: Error validating Y-fields: {e}")

        # Validate position bounds
        layout = config.layout
        row, col = plot_spec.position
        if row >= layout.rows or col >= layout.cols:
            errors.append(
                f"Plot {i + 1}: Position ({row}, {col}) exceeds layout bounds "
                f"({layout.rows - 1}, {layout.cols - 1})"
            )

    return errors

371	def get_scenario_data(
372	    run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
373	) -> Tuple[Dict[str, List[int]], List[Dict[int, MetricsData]], List[str]]:
374	    """
375	    Collects all traffic scenarios and returns the required data structures for
376	    plotting.
377	
378	    Args:
379	        run_data_list (list): List of tuples containing run data and experiment
380	        metadata.
381	
382	    Returns:
383	        tuple:
384	            - label_to_concurrency_map (dict): A dictionary mapping each
385	              traffic scenario  label (as a string) to a sorted list of
386	              concurrency levels (integers) associated with that scenario.
387	              The key is in the format `"Scenario: <scenario_name>"`.
388	
389	            - concurrency_data_list (list): A list of dictionaries, where each
390	              dictionary contains the run data (metrics) for a specific
391	              scenario across various concurrency levels. Each item in this
392	              list corresponds to one traffic scenario, and the dictionary keys
393	              represent different concurrency levels, while the values contain
394	              the aggregated and individual metrics at each level.
395	
396	            - labels (list): A list of labels for each traffic scenario,
397	              formatted as `"Scenario: <scenario_name>"`. Each label in this
398	              list directly matches the keys in `label_to_concurrency_map` and
399	              is used for labeling plots.
400	
401	    """
402	    label_to_concurrency_map = {}
403	    concurrency_data_list = []
404	    labels = []
405	    for metadata, run_data in run_data_list:
406	        for scenario, concurrency_data in run_data.items():
407	            label = f"Scenario: {scenario}"
408	            label_to_concurrency_map[label] = sorted(concurrency_data.keys())
409	            concurrency_data_list.append(concurrency_data)
410	            labels.append(label)
411	    return label_to_concurrency_map, concurrency_data_list, labels
412	
413	
414	def get_group_data(
415	    run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
416	    traffic_scenario: str,
417	    group_key: str,
418	) -> Tuple[Dict[str, List[int]], List[Dict[int, MetricsData]], List[str]]:
419	    """
420	    Collects data grouped by a specific group key for a particular traffic
421	    scenario.
422	
423	    Args:
424	        run_data_list (list): List of tuples containing run data and experiment
425	            metadata.
426	        traffic_scenario (str): The traffic scenario to filter by.
427	        group_key (str): The group key to use for grouping the data.
428	
429	    Returns:
430	        tuple: A tuple containing label-to-concurrency mapping, concurrency
431	            data list, and labels.
432	    """
433	    label_to_concurrency_map = {}
434	    concurrency_data_list = []
435	    labels = []
436	    for metadata, run_data in run_data_list:
437	        if traffic_scenario not in run_data:
438	            continue
439	        group_label = getattr(metadata, group_key, "Unknown")
440	
441	        # extra base folder name from abspath
442	        if group_key == "experiment_folder_name":
443	            group_label = os.path.basename(group_label)
444	
445	        label = f"{group_key}: {group_label}"
446	        label_to_concurrency_map[label] = sorted(run_data[traffic_scenario].keys())
447	        concurrency_data_list.append(run_data[traffic_scenario])
448	        labels.append(label)
449	    return label_to_concurrency_map, concurrency_data_list, labels
450	
451	
452	def extract_traffic_scenarios(
453	    run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
454	) -> set:
455	    """
456	    Extracts a set of unique traffic scenarios from the run data list.
457	
458	    Args:
459	        run_data_list (list): List of tuples containing run data and experiment
460	            metadata.
461	
462	    Returns:
463	        set: A set of unique traffic scenarios found in the run data.
464	    """
465	    traffic_scenarios: set[str] = set()
466	    for _, run_data in run_data_list:
467	        traffic_scenarios.update(run_data.keys())
468	    return traffic_scenarios
469	
470	
471	def finalize_and_save_plots(
472	    axs: Any,
473	    fig: Figure,
474	    labels: List[str],
475	    experiment_folder: str,
476	    output_file_prefix: str,
477	) -> None:
478	    """
479	    Finalizes plot settings and saves each plot as individual images and a
480	    combined image.
481	
482	    Args:
483	        axs (array-like): Array of matplotlib axes from the main figure.
484	        fig (Figure): The main matplotlib figure object containing the subplots.
485	        labels (list): List of labels for the plot legend.
486	        experiment_folder (str): Path to the folder where the plots will be
487	            saved.
488	        output_file_prefix (str): Prefix for the output file names.
489	    """
490	    save_individual_subplots(axs, experiment_folder, output_file_prefix)
491	
492	    fig.legend(labels=labels, loc="lower center", ncol=3, fancybox=True)
493	    plt.tight_layout()
494	    plt.subplots_adjust(bottom=0.1)
495	
496	    output_file = os.path.join(
497	        experiment_folder, f"{output_file_prefix}_combined_plots_2x4.png"
498	    )
499	    logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")
500	    plt.savefig(output_file)
501	    plt.close()
502	
503	
504	def save_individual_subplots(
505	    axs: Any, experiment_folder: str, output_file_prefix: str
506	) -> None:
507	    """
508	    Extracts and saves each subplot from a populated 2x3 grid figure as
509	    individual PNGs, using each subplot's title as the filename.
510	
511	    Args:
512	        axs (array-like): The array of matplotlib axes from the main figure.
513	        experiment_folder (str): Directory path to save each individual subplot.
514	        output_file_prefix (str): Prefix of the output file to save individual.
515	    """
516	    for ax in axs.flat:
517	        title = ax.get_title()
518	        sanitized_title = sanitize_string(title)
519	
520	        fig_temp, ax_temp = plt.subplots()
521	
522	        # Copy the content of the original axis to the new axis
523	        for line in ax.get_lines():
524	            ax_temp.plot(
525	                line.get_xdata(),
526	                line.get_ydata(),
527	                label=line.get_label(),
528	                marker=line.get_marker(),
529	            )
530	
531	        # Copy each annotation from the original axis to the new axis
532	        for annotation in ax.texts:
533	            ax_temp.annotate(
534	                annotation.get_text(),
535	                xy=annotation.get_position(),
536	                fontsize=annotation.get_fontsize(),
537	                ha=annotation.get_ha(),
538	                va=annotation.get_va(),
539	            )
540	
541	        # Copy scale settings
542	        if ax.get_yscale() == "log":
543	            ax_temp.set_yscale("log")
544	
545	        # Copy axis limits
546	        ax_temp.set_xlim(ax.get_xlim())
547	        ax_temp.set_ylim(ax.get_ylim())
548	
549	        ax_temp.set_title(title)
550	        ax_temp.set_xlabel(ax.get_xlabel())
551	        ax_temp.set_xticks(ax.get_xticks())
552	        ax_temp.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels())
553	        ax_temp.set_ylabel(ax.get_ylabel())
554	        ax_temp.grid(ax.get_xgridlines())
555	        ax_temp.minorticks_on()
556	
557	        # Calculate max_label_length based on figure width
558	        fig_width = fig_temp.get_figwidth()
559	        max_label_length = int(fig_width * 10)
560	
561	        handles, labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
562	        if handles and labels:
563	            # Wrap each label if it exceeds the calculated max_label_length
564	            wrapped_labels = [
565	                "\n".join(textwrap.wrap(label, max_label_length)) for label in labels
566	            ]
567	            ax_temp.legend(handles, wrapped_labels, fontsize="x-small", fancybox=True)
568	
569	        fig_temp.tight_layout()
570	        output_file = os.path.join(
571	            experiment_folder, f"{output_file_prefix}_{sanitized_title}.png"
572	        )
573	        fig_temp.savefig(output_file)
574	        plt.close(fig_temp)
575	        logger.info(f"🎨 Saving {output_file}")
576	
577	
578	def plot_single_scenario_inference_speed_vs_throughput(
579	    scenario_label: str,
580	    experiment_folder: str,
581	    task: str,
582	    scenario_metrics: Dict[str, Any],
583	    iteration_type: str,
584	    metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
585	) -> None:
586	    """
587	    Plots metrics for a single scenario immediately after it completes.
588	
589	    Args:
590	        scenario_label (str): Label for the scenario being plotted
591	        experiment_folder (str): Path to save the plot
592	        task: The benchmark task e.g. text-to-text
593	        scenario_metrics: A dict with two keys:
594	            "Data": Dictionary containing metrics data for different concurrency levels
595	                or batch
596	             iteration_type: List of concurrency levels/batch
597	        iteration_type: Type of iteration for the benchmark. E.g. concurrency levels.
598	        metrics_time_unit (str):
599	            [s|ms] Time unit for latency metrics display and export.
600	    """
601	    # TODO: This logic should be de-coupled.
602	    if (
603	        task.split("-to-")[-1] == "embeddings" or task.split("-to-")[-1] == "rerank"
604	    ) and not scenario_metrics["data"]:
605	        return
606	
607	    concurrency_data = scenario_metrics["data"]
608	    concurrency_levels = sorted(scenario_metrics[f"{iteration_type}"])
609	    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
610	
611	    # Filter out concurrency levels with missing data and collect valid data points
612	    valid_x_data = []
613	    valid_y_data = []
614	    valid_concurrency = []
615	
616	    for c in concurrency_levels:
617	        try:
618	            speed = concurrency_data[c][
619	                "aggregated_metrics"
620	            ].stats.output_inference_speed.mean
621	            output_throughput = concurrency_data[c][
622	                "aggregated_metrics"
623	            ].mean_output_throughput_tokens_per_s
624	
625	            if speed is not None and output_throughput is not None:
626	                valid_y_data.append(speed)
627	                valid_x_data.append(output_throughput)
628	                valid_concurrency.append(c)
629	        except (KeyError, AttributeError) as e:
630	            logger.warning(
631	                f"Missing inference speed data for concurrency level {c} in scenario "
632	                f"{scenario_label}: {str(e)}"
633	            )
634	            continue
635	
636	    if not valid_y_data or not valid_x_data:
637	        logger.warning(
638	            f"No valid inference speed data found for any concurrency level in "
639	            f"scenario {scenario_label}"
640	        )
641	        plt.close()
642	        return
643	
644	    # Plot using only valid data points
645	    plot_graph(
646	        ax=ax,
647	        x_data=valid_x_data,
648	        y_data=valid_y_data,
649	        x_label="Output Throughput of Server (tokens/s)",
650	        y_label="Output Inference Speed per Request (tokens/s)",
651	        title=f"Output Inference Speed per Request vs "
652	        f"Output Throughput of Server - {scenario_label}",
653	        concurrency_levels=valid_concurrency,
654	        label=f"Scenario: {scenario_label}",
655	        plot_type="line",
656	        metrics_time_unit=metrics_time_unit,
657	    )
658	
659	    ax.legend()
660	    ax.minorticks_on()
661	    plt.tight_layout()
662	
663	    output_file = os.path.join(
664	        experiment_folder,
665	        f"interim_{sanitize_string(scenario_label)}_output_speed_vs_throughput.png",
666	    )
667	    logger.info(f"🎨 Saving interim plot: {output_file}")
668	    plt.savefig(output_file)
669	    plt.close()
670	
671	
672	def plot_error_rates(
673	    ax: Axes,
674	    concurrency_data: Dict[int, dict],
675	    concurrency_levels: List[int],
676	    label: str,
677	) -> None:
678	    """
679	    Creates a stacked bar plot of error rates by actual HTTP status code
680	    across concurrency levels. Uses http.HTTPStatus to label each code.
681	
682	    Args:
683	        ax (Axes): Matplotlib axis to plot on
684	        concurrency_data (dict): Dictionary containing error data for each concurrency
685	        concurrency_levels (list): List of concurrency levels
686	        label (str): Label for the legend
687	    """
688	    all_codes = set()
689	    for c in concurrency_levels:
690	        aggregated = concurrency_data[c]["aggregated_metrics"]
691	        error_freq = aggregated.error_codes_frequency
692	        all_codes.update(error_freq.keys())
693	
694	    # Convert to a sorted list so each code is consistently stacked
695	    all_codes = sorted(all_codes)  # type: ignore[assignment]
696	
697	    bottom = np.zeros(len(concurrency_levels))
698	    color_map = plt.get_cmap("tab20")
699	    existing_handles, existing_labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
700	
701	    for idx, code in enumerate(all_codes):
702	        # Use HTTPStatus to get a nice textual label, e.g. "404 Not Found".
703	        # If Python's HTTPStatus doesn't recognize it, just show the numeric code.
704	        try:
705	            code_label = f"{code} {HTTPStatus(code).phrase}"
706	        except ValueError:
707	            code_label = str(code)
708	
709	        error_rates = []
710	        for c_index, c_val in enumerate(concurrency_levels):
711	            aggregated = concurrency_data[c_val]["aggregated_metrics"]
712	            total_requests = aggregated.num_requests
713	            freq_dict = aggregated.error_codes_frequency
714	
715	            count_this_code = freq_dict.get(code, 0)
716	            error_rate = count_this_code / total_requests if total_requests > 0 else 0
717	            error_rates.append(error_rate)
718	
719	        ax.bar(
720	            concurrency_levels,
721	            error_rates,
722	            bottom=bottom,
723	            color=color_map(idx / len(all_codes)),
724	            label=code_label,
725	        )
726	
727	        bottom += np.array(error_rates)
728	
729	    ax.set_xlabel("Concurrency")
730	    ax.set_ylabel("Error Rate")
731	    ax.set_title("Error Rates by HTTP Status vs Concurrency")
732	    # Re-enable autoscale for y so subsequent groups can extend the top,
733	    # then pin bottom at 0 (valid for linear scale used here)
734	    ax.autoscale(enable=True, axis="y", tight=False)
735	    y_bottom, y_top = ax.get_ylim()
736	    ax.set_ylim(bottom=0.0, top=y_top)
737	    ax.legend()
738	    ax.grid(True)
739
